description: A dish of finely diced raw tuna seasoned with ingredients such as citrus, soy sauce, herbs, or sesame and served chilled. It is valued for freshness, texture, and clean flavor.
core_ingredients: Acid such as citrus or vinegar
preparation_steps: Use very fresh, properly handled tuna suitable for raw preparation.
Dice the tuna evenly and keep it chilled.
Gently season just before serving so the texture stays delicate.
Plate with garnishes or crisp accompaniments.

health_advice.benefits: High in protein and usually lighter than fried or creamy appetizers.
health_advice.risks: Because it is served raw, strict freshness, cold-chain handling, and food safety are essential.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Only use raw fish from trusted sources and avoid leaving it unrefrigerated.

dietary_notes.note: Plain tuna tartare can be gluten-free, but soy sauce and some garnishes may contain gluten.
